stimulus: Jazz pianist Mary Lou Williams’s 1942 arrival in New York City marked a pivotal moment in her career. It was there that Williams composed her Zodiac Suite—a work that drew praise for fusing classical orchestration with bebop harmonies—and ______blank mentorships with younger musicians such as Thelonious Monk and Bud Powell.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The missing verb is part of the same clause as the verb "composed," and "Williams" is the subject of both. So we need the past-tense form "cultivated" in order to match "composed."

Choice B is incorrect. This creates a verb-form error; a finite past-tense verb is needed to match the parallel main verb "composed." Choice C is incorrect. This creates a verb-form error; a finite past-tense verb is needed. Choice D is incorrect. This creates a verb-form error; a finite past-tense verb is needed to match "composed."
